March 2026

Project
Proposal

Helping Ian Lowes turn two decades of unmatched evidence into a marketing machine that makes the market finally pay attention.

Presented to:
Ian Lowes / Autocalls.uk
Presented by:
Test Tube Marketing

The problem.

Ian,

I described what you're going through on our call as being like someone who's found the fountain of youth and is standing there going "everyone, come and have a drink" - and they're walking right past you.

You said: "You'd end up with a reputation that the man's obsessed. Would you not see why?"

I do see why. And the frustrating thing is - this isn't even a close call.

Over a billion pounds of autocall contracts traded through your advisory business. 99.7% positive outcomes across more than 2,000 maturities over a decade. Consistent 7-8% annual returns through Brexit, a pandemic, inflationary shocks, tariff chaos, and multiple market corrections - without anyone having to get up in the morning to pick the right shares at the right time. A 10-year track record on the 10:10 Plan that has substantially outperformed the equity income funds that journalist told you she'd rather buy. And you've now built the digital transaction platform that removes the paper-based barrier that's held this entire sector back for 20 years.

You don't have a product problem. You don't have a credibility problem. You don't have a performance problem.

You have a 'nobody knows about it' problem.

And that's a marketing problem. Which means it's fixable.

What I took from our conversation.

A few things stood out.

You're a tester, not a gambler. You said it yourself - you've always been one for test marketing. You're not about to hand a large sum to an agency and say "bugger off and get on with it." You want to find where the explanation fails, where the video isn't landing, where the proper touchpoint is. That's the right instinct, and it's how we'd approach this anyway.

You know the product converts face to face. Twenty years of sitting in front of clients and it was never a problem. The challenge is translating that for people who aren't already in the room with you. That gap - between what you know and what the market hears - is exactly what we'd be working on.

Phase one is IFAs. Not retail. Not yet. Retail transacting is 6-12 months away pending regulatory permissions. But IFAs are the gatekeepers for now, and they need evidence-based marketing - not hype. The good news is you've got more evidence than anyone else in the sector. You've been doing it since 2003.

The scale of what you're building is significant. The banks need £5 billion pounds of transactions in five years to take this seriously. But you're not expecting trades tomorrow - initial success looks like activity and volume. IFA enquiries. Registrations. Evidence that the message travels. That's exactly the right starting point.

You've been approached by other agencies. Including one with financial services expertise. You also said you'd rather support the smaller, scrappier operation - but you need to be able to pitch "why them, not the bigger one" to your colleagues. I'll address that directly later in this document.

"Initial success is activity and volume. Not transactions. Not £5 billion overnight. Proof that the message works outside of a face-to-face conversation."

What's missing.

Right now you've got two strong brands (Autocalls.uk and The 10:10 Plan) with no active marketing behind either. A live platform with simulators, virtuals, and end-to-end digital transaction capability - currently only collecting expressions of interest. Some trade publication mentions and LinkedIn activity. A team of four, supplemented by Fiverr freelancers. A compliance requirement that means every financial promotion needs sign-off before it goes anywhere. No coherent marketing strategy connecting any of this together.

What's missing is someone to take what's in your head - and in your data - and turn it into a systematic marketing operation that works in sequence, produces content that passes compliance, and tests what actually resonates before you spend serious money scaling it.

What we'd do.

Four workstreams, running in parallel across 90 days.

Workstream 1
Messaging and Positioning Foundation

This is the critical first piece. We start by constructing the core message architecture - the version of the autocall story that works in 60 seconds, in a LinkedIn post, in an email subject line, and in a three-minute video. We'll work with everything you've already produced and strip it back to the essentials a busy IFA will actually engage with.

Some of the raw material is already strong. The "between cash and shares" positioning. The "22 days in history where it hasn't worked" stat. The Lehman Brothers rebuttal. The fact that nobody has to get up in the morning to buy the right shares. These are good angles. They just need shaping and sequencing for someone who hasn't been living in this world for two decades.

The output is a messaging playbook - a document the whole team can use and that compliance can review once and sign off on as a foundation for everything else.

Workstream 2
IFA-Facing Email Campaign

IFAs aren't going to find you on social media. They need to be reached directly. We build a curated outreach campaign targeting IFA firms - short, evidence-led emails that land in an inbox and feel like they've been written by someone who actually understands what they're talking about.

The goal isn't to close on the first email. It's to get a response, a registration on the platform, or a conversation. We write it, you and IDAD compliance-clear it, we send it.

Workstream 3
LinkedIn Content and Thought Leadership

We build a consistent content calendar - two to three posts per week - that positions you as the sector's independent authority on autocalls. Your annual performance data. Myth-busting around the Lehman Brothers hangover. The 22 days stat. All drafted by us, compliance-cleared by you and IDAD, and posted consistently. Over 90 days, this builds the ambient credibility that makes cold outreach land better.

Workstream 4
IFA Landing Page and Video

We build a dedicated landing page aimed squarely at IFAs - clean, evidence-led, and designed to do the job your face-to-face meetings have been doing for 20 years. Plus a scripted 2-3 minute video that sits on that page and does the heavy lifting of explanation. Not a knocked-up version - the polished, strategically scripted version built on what we learn from Workstream 1.

What success looks like.

You said it on the call: initial success is activity and volume. Not transactions. Not £5 billion overnight. Proof that the message works outside of a face-to-face conversation. Here's a realistic range for what a 90-day engagement could produce:

MetricTarget Range
IFA firms contacted via email campaign200-400
Email open rate35-50%
IFA responses / platform registrations20-50
LinkedIn follower growth300-600
LinkedIn post impressions per week5,000-15,000

These are proof-of-concept numbers. The point is to demonstrate that the message travels - and to identify which version of it travels best. That's your test marketing phase.

What you get.

  • Full messaging and positioning playbook - compliant-ready, usable across all channels
  • IFA outreach list - 200-400 qualified contacts with decision-maker details
  • 3-part email sequence - written and formatted for compliance review
  • LinkedIn content - 2-3 posts per week for 90 days, drafted and scheduled
  • Short-form video scripts - for your existing footage and any new content
  • IFA-facing landing page - copy, structure, and design direction
  • Scripted and produced short-form VSL - 2-3 minutes for the landing page
  • Weekly progress updates
  • WhatsApp access - to me and the team, same-day responses

Everything created is yours. No lock-in after the initial 90-day term.

Your investment.

Given that you've described this as a test phase - and given your sensible instinct to prove the model before scaling it - I'd suggest a focused 90-day engagement rather than an open-ended retainer. Defined scope, clear budget, and a point at which to evaluate before committing further.

90-Day Engagement

Month 1 — Strategy, Messaging & Foundation£2,995+VAT
Months 2-3 — Execution & Testing£2,495+VAT/mo
Total (90 days)£7,985+VAT

For context: this is less than you'd pay a mid-level marketing manager for three months - and you get a team, not an individual. No recruitment, no notice period, no HR headaches. You told me you don't want to open the door on hiring a marketing manager because "soon as I open that door, they need an assistant, then someone else, then a specialist." This avoids all of that.

Why us, not them.

You asked me directly: why Test Tube Marketing and not one of the bigger agencies, including the one with financial services expertise who's already circling.

Here's the honest answer.

The agency with FS expertise has a relationship advantage - they know the compliance landscape. That's genuinely valuable. But what they're likely to give you is a strategy document. A plan. A set of recommendations. And then it's on you and your team of four to figure out how to execute it.

We don't do that. We do the work. The writing, the outreach, the content creation, the testing - all of it. In-house, UK-based team. No freelancers, no subcontractors.

You told me you bought one of those AI-powered "12 employees" systems and had it refunded within three days because the output was rubbish. You mentioned the Fiverr freelancers - spending £500 on a video and then hours on edit instructions. That's the world you're trying to get out of. We're the alternative.

We've generated over £36.2 million in revenue for clients over 10 years. We won't pretend we have deep structured products expertise - you know more about autocalls than we ever will. But we understand how to take a complicated proposition, find the human story inside it, and get it in front of the right people in a way that builds trust. That's the gap you've got.

The compliance piece is a reality we take seriously. Everything we produce goes through your sign-off process before it goes anywhere.

Next steps.

  1. 1
    Have a read of this before Thursday. If the approach makes sense, we can discuss scope, timeline, and any questions on the day. If anything doesn't land right, tell me - I'd rather know.
  2. 2
    We meet Thursday the 19th at midday - you, your colleague, and us.
  3. 3
    If we're aligned, we agree scope and kick off Workstream 1 within the week.
  4. 4
    90 days later, the message travels - and we have data to tell us how to scale.

Speak Thursday.

Fish (Nick Fisher) Test Tube Marketing
bigfish@testtubemarking.com

About Test Tube
Marketing

Hi, we're Ad and Fish, the founders of Test Tube Marketing.

Between us, we've been running marketing campaigns, building funnels, and making sales online for over 25 years.

And nowadays we spend our time helping our clients get great results (and drinking coffee).

Unlike other agencies, who will try and force your business to fit their model, we take a completely unique approach to every client we work with.

Ultimately, our experienced team and us take the time to understand your business, your goals, and your unique skills, to ensure we can deliver the results you're looking for.
